author: mark
text:
Anchor [minor]/[major] regex at whitespace boundaries

custom_minor_increment_regex / custom_major_increment_regex were just '\[minor\]' / '\[major\]' — a literal substring search. bl-313a's own commit message ("Auto-derive minor/major bumps from [minor]/[major] markers [bl-313a]") contains the literal string [minor] embedded in the prose '[minor]/[major]' and self-matched, so release-plz proposed v0.5.0 for the post-0.4.0 window even though no commit intended a minor bump.

Require start-of-string-or-whitespace before and end-of-string-or-whitespace after the bracketed token, so the marker has to be a standalone bracketed word in the message. Prose like '[minor]/[major] markers' (slash-joined, no boundary whitespace) no longer matches; real uses like 'Add forge-gated delivery mode [minor]' still do, including markers in commit bodies after a newline.

Update release-plz.toml's comment to spell out the boundary requirement and update README's bump-signaling section so future commits documenting the marker convention don't recur the self-trigger.
